title: workspace.v1
description: A workspace is a USER git repo (the agent's durable memory) — data, not platform code. vexa owns ONLY the init template (the repo layout, see README) + this BASE entity-frontmatter convention. The kg/ subtree is an Open Knowledge Format (OKF) v0.1 bundle (github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/tree/main/okf): OKF requires only `type`; we additionally require `id` + `title` (a stricter, still-conformant superset — OKF consumers must tolerate extra keys). Customers extend the frontmatter per entity type (P11: the entity schema is config, not platform). Not a wire payload.

EntityFrontmatter.description: The typed YAML frontmatter atop a markdown entity (kg/entities/<type>/<slug>.md). OKF v0.1 superset: `type` is OKF's sole required key; `title`, `description`, `resource`, `tags`, `timestamp` are OKF's recommended keys. Extensible — customers add fields per entity type. Reserved OKF files (index.md, log.md) carry NO frontmatter and are not entities.

type.description: entity type, e.g. contact | company | meeting (OKF: the only required key)

description.description: OKF recommended: one-line summary of the concept

resource.description: OKF recommended: URI of the external system-of-record this concept describes

timestamp.description: OKF recommended: last-modification time of the knowledge in this document
